channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: A Russian bucket brigade helps toads and frogs cross the road to get to a spawning site
item.description: A NATURE RESERVE NEAR SESTRORETSK, Russia (AP) — It happens every spring along a section of road north of Russia's second-largest city of St. Petersburg: Volunteers, many in yellow vests, patrol near the Sestroretsk Bog nature reserve.
item.dc:creator: KIRILL ZARUBIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Burchett signals support for sending US criminals to El Salvador
item.description: Rep. Tim Burchett (R-Tenn.) on Wednesday defended President Trump’s suggestion that U.S. citizens convicted of violent crimes be sent to Salvadoran prisons. “They're criminals. They broke our laws.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Ukraine's future is at the center of talks in Paris with Rubio, Witkoff and top European officials
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Paris is hosting a series of talks Thursday about Ukraine and its security, including U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio and presidential envoy Steve Witkoff, French President Emmanuel Macron and top Ukrainian and European officials.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: UN nuclear watchdog says US-Iran talks at a 'very crucial' stage
item.description: Talks between Iran and the United States over Tehran’s rapidly advancing nuclear program are “in a very crucial” stage.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell And Amir Vahdat, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Portrait of Palestinian boy who lost both arms is World Press Photo of the year for 2025
item.description: A portrait of a young Palestinian boy who lost both arms as a result of an Israeli attack in Gaza has been named World Press Photo of the year.
item.dc:creator: Mike Corder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Puerto Rico hit with massive island-wide blackout ahead of Easter weekend
item.description: A massive island-wide blackout hit the U.S. territory of Puerto Rico midday Wednesday as residents were preparing for the Easter weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Divided loyalties? 62 Taiwanese military members found with Chinese residency permits
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — At least 62 active-duty Taiwanese military members have been found holding Chinese residency permits, the self-ruled island's Defense Ministry said, in the latest revelation of Chinese influence in the Taiwanese armed forces.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Carney says Trump key issue in Canada's election, while Conservative rival says country needs change
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Prime Minister Mark Carney said Wednesday the key question in Canada's election is who is best to deal with U.S. President Donald Trump while his Conservative rival argued that Carney doesn't represent change after a decade of Liberal Party rule.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: North Korea threatens a response to US flying long-range bombers over South Korea
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea on Thursday threatened unspecified retaliation after the U.S. flew long-range bombers over South Korea during training with South Korean warplanes, which North Korea views as practice for an attack against it. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dog reunites with Israeli family after disappearing for 18 months in Gaza
item.description: BINYAMINA, Israel (AP) — Rachel Dancyg never thought she would see her dog again after it disappeared in the Hamas attack that sparked the ongoing war with Israel. Her ex-husband and brother were abducted from Kibbutz Nir Oz in southern Israel and killed.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Puerto Rico goes dark after massive power outage
item.description: Puerto Rico went dark on Wednesday after experiencing a massive power outage on the island impacting families, businesses and homes alike.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Van Hollen denied meeting, phone call with Abrego Garcia during El Salvador visit
item.description: The Salvadoran government on Wednesday rebuffed Sen. Chris Van Hollen’s (D-Md.) request to meet or speak with a wrongfully deported Maryland man, the senator said, accusing the Trump administration and others of lying about Kilmar Abrego Garcia’s gang ties.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: UK's top court says definition of a woman is based on biological sex and excludes transgender people
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The U.K. Supreme Court ruled Wednesday that a woman is someone born biologically female, excluding transgender people from the legal definition in a long-running dispute between a feminist group and the Scottish government.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, JILL LAWLESS and SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, The Hill

item.title: WTO warns of global trade decline amid Trump tariff war
item.description: The WTO has deemed the impact of Trump's tariff overhaul and ongoing uncertainty a "crisis" and warned that if the situation deteriorates, trade flows could be reduced, leading to a 0.2 percent fall in global merchandise trade in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Vance to visit leaders of Italy, India
item.description: Vice President Vance will travel next week to Italy and India to meet with leaders there and visit cultural sites. Vance will be joined by second lady Usha Vance and their children for the trip, which will begin Friday with a trip to Italy.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Shootout rescues Tennessee pastor abducted in South Africa
item.description: Josh Sullivan, a self-described “church-planting missionary,” has been reunited with his wife and their two children.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Maryland man's family questions death, says he didn't drown in Bahamas
item.description: Dinari McAlmont of Bowie, Maryland, was found dead April 5 near the Atlantis resort on Paradise Island, according to Royal Bahamian Police.
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation

item.title: In the Ukrainian city of Sumy, life goes on despite the constant threat of attack
item.description: SUMY, Ukraine (AP) — The humdrum of daily life in Sumy belies the constant threat of death its people have lived with since Russia invaded Ukraine three years ago.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and YEHOR KONOVALOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NATO chief reaffirms support for Ukraine during a visit to the port city of Odesa
item.description: ODESA, Ukraine (AP) — NATO’s support for Ukraine remains “unwavering,” the alliance’s secretary-general said Tuesday, emphasizing that more than 20 billion euros — over $22 billion — in security assistance has already been pledged by NATO allies in the first three months of the year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Four journalists who were accused of working for Kremlin foe Navalny are convicted of extremism
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — A Russian court on Tuesday convicted four journalists of extremism for working for an anti-corruption group founded by the late opposition leader Alexei Navalny and sentenced them to 5 1/2 years in prison each.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian general who criticized leadership may soon lead battalion of ex-cons: lawyer
item.description: Russia may soon reinstate a general who criticized Russia's top military leadership for blunders in Ukraine on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Father of American-Israeli hostage pushes for ceasefire
item.description: Adi Alexander shares a message for his son, urging him to stay strong and assuring him the family won’t stop fighting for his return.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas says it's sending a delegation to Qatar to continue Gaza ceasefire talks
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — A Hamas official said Monday that the Palestinian militant group is sending a delegation to the Gulf state of Qatar to continue indirect ceasefire talks with Israel over the war in Gaza, as the territory’s Health Ministry said that 38 people were confirmed dead over
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel intensifies strikes across Gaza on Palm Sunday and hits a hospital in the north
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza (AP) — A wave of Israeli strikes across Gaza on Sunday hit a hospital, a municipal building, a home and a vehicle, killing at least 21 people, including children, as Israel vowed to expand its security presence in the small coastal strip.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: More than 30 Ukrainians killed by Russian ballistics in Palm Sunday strike
item.description: Russia hit the Ukrainian city of Sumy with two missiles as civilian celebrated Palm Sunday, killing more than 30 people and injuring dozens of others, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fears of another narco-state rise as Latin American country readies for pivotal vote
item.description: Ecuador is headed to a second round of a presidential election in which incumbent President Daniel Noboa faces leftist challenger Luisa González as the nation faces gang violence.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel cuts off Gaza's southern city of Rafah and vows to 'vigorously' expand in the territory
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel announced Saturday it had completed construction of a new security corridor cutting off the southern city of Rafah from the rest of Gaza, and the military said it would soon expand “vigorously” in most of the small coastal territory.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: With Trump's backing Israel pushes deeper into Gaza as pressure builds for hostage deal
item.description: An Israeli official says military operations and hostage talks are fully coordinated with the U.S., as officials remain optimistic about a deal. Israel continues its advance in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Delegates from Iran, US holding talks in Oman amid ongoing tensions: What to know
item.description: Delegates from the United States and Iran are holding talks in Oman Saturday in a delicate effort to restart negotiations over Tehran’s controversial nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico to make ‘immediate’ water payment to US as Trump threatens sanctions
item.description: Hours after President Donald Trump threatened Mexico with additional tariffs over a massive water debt, that country's president publicly vowed to make a substantial payment soon.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Trump admin ordered to return alleged MS-13 member mistakenly deported
item.description: The justices did not say exactly what Kilmar Abrego Garcia's release would entail or how he would return to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Exclusive: Prince Harry, King Charles' rift widens; Charles refused to see Harry in UK
item.description: Prince Harry also raised eyebrows this weekend when he missed the wedding of close friend, Lord Vivian.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation
